content.question: Does Hungary's current USEMI (Unified Special Educational, Conductive Educational Methodological Institutions) system function more like a diagnosis-gated entitlement model (England/Israel pattern) or a tiered needs-responsive model (Finland pattern), and what are the specific finance and placement rules that structure identification incentives?
content.reduction_path: Conduct detailed institutional audit of Hungarian USEMI funding architecture: which entitlements are diagnosis-gated vs. needs-assessed; what are the threshold rules for moving between mainstream, resource, and special placements; what is the distribution of support-access across these pathways by diagnostic category and county.
